axis_ceiling_verdict.reason: The defensive trade floor remains unsatisfiable on the bounded no-trade continuation-release axis even before churn is considered.

findings: The bounded no-trade release axis cannot satisfy the full replay-quality gate on its own under unchanged baseline and first-revision inputs.
The defensive trade floor remains short by 8 trades even after the first revision, which keeps a separate bottleneck outside the continuation-release axis in place.
The change-rate ceiling still leaves capacity for at most 7 additional actual policy changes above baseline, so churn is binding but not the only blocker on this axis.

actual_policy_change_rate_ceiling.axis_reason: Change-rate pressure is binding but controllable by selecting a smaller subset of candidate releases on the same axis.

continuation_trade_floor.axis_reason: The bounded no-trade release axis directly increases continuation participation and the floor is already satisfied in baseline.

defensive_trade_floor.axis_reason: The bounded no-trade release axis only releases RI_no_trade_policy -> RI_continuation_policy rows, so defensive trade count remains invariant on this axis.

proposed_followup_hypothesis: A future slice should investigate the defensive-policy scarcity bottleneck separately from the no-trade continuation-release axis.
